topic: Windsurf Memories Guide 2026: How to Make Cascade Remember Your Codebase

competitors.title: Windsurf Memories — Official Documentation
key_points: Memories are auto-generated by Cascade during conversations; stored locally in ~/.codeium/windsurf/memories/
Workspace-specific: each project has its own memory store
Don't consume credits — memories are free to create and use
Users can manually ask Cascade to 'create a memory of…' for explicit persistence

Comparison table of 5 context systems: Rules (coding conventions, always_on/glob/model_decision/manual), AGENTS.md (location-scoped, zero config), Workflows (prompt templates, slash command), Skills (multi-step with supporting files), Memories (auto-context, automatic retrieval)
Rules scopes: Global (6000 char limit), Workspace (.windsurf/rules/*.md, 12000 chars/file), AGENTS.md (root=always-on, subdirectory=auto-glob), System/Enterprise (OS-specific paths)
Recommendation: for durable knowledge, prefer Rules or AGENTS.md over auto-generated Memories

competitors.title: Windsurf Rules Guide - AI Configuration | design.dev
key_points: Rules vs Memories distinction: Rules are developer-authored, version-controlled, shared; Memories are auto-captured, not version-controlled, personal to each developer
Use Rules for: coding standards, architecture decisions, framework conventions, team-wide standards
Use Memories for: project-specific learned context, personal preferences, dynamic evolving context, details you don't need to explicitly document
Modern format: .windsurf/rules/ directory with per-rule .md files vs legacy .windsurfrules single file
Migration strategy: split single .windsurfrules into logical grouped files under .windsurf/rules/
Wave 8 introduced modern rule format with YAML frontmatter per rule file
Activation modes: Always Apply, Manual, Model Decision, Glob (pattern-matched)
Workspace rules committed to Git enable team sharing — major advantage of Rules over Memories
Best practice: avoid generic rules, use clear formatting with bullet points, group with XML tags, stay under 12,000 chars per file

competitors.title: .windsurfrules Guide: Configure Windsurf AI (2026)
key_points: .windsurfrules is configuration file for Windsurf AI (Codeium/now part of OpenAI) providing instructions to Cascade
Rule file locations: project root (.windsurfrules), workspace rules (.windsurf/rules/), global rules (global_rules.md), Settings UI
Rules use Markdown with XML tags to group related rules (tech_stack, coding_guidelines, naming_conventions, testing, do_not)
4 activation modes: Always Apply, Manual, Model Decision, Glob
Best practices: avoid generic rules, use clear formatting, group with XML tags, stay under 12,000 characters, be specific about versions
Cross-tool comparison available: .windsurfrules vs .cursorrules vs CLAUDE.md vs GEMINI.md

competitors.title: Windsurf AI IDE Statistics 2026: Users, Revenue & Adoption
key_points: Windsurf has 1M+ active users and 70M+ lines of AI code per day
94% of code Windsurf produces is AI-written — AI-native IDE positioning
4,000+ enterprises in production, 59% of Fortune 500 companies building with Windsurf
Reuters reported $82M ARR and 350+ enterprise customers in July 2025
TechCrunch reported ~$40M ARR (Feb 2025) to ~$100M ARR (Apr 2025) showing rapid growth
Pricing: Free $0, Pro $15/month, Teams $30/user/month, Enterprise custom
Rebranded from Codeium in April 2025; $3B reported OpenAI acquisition; Cognition acquisition after Google's $2.4B deal
